data.title: Cadence Stock Surges After Q4 Beat, Strong Outlook
data.description:
Cadence Design Systems, Inc. shares are trading higher Wednesday after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results and issued first-quarter adjusted earnings per share guidance above estimates on Tuesday after the market closed.

  • Cadence stock is surging to new heights today. Why is CDNS stock up today?

Fourth-Quarter Results And Guidance

Cadence reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.99, beating the consensus estimate of $1.91. In addition, the company reported revenue of $1.44 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $1.42 billion.

Revenue increased 14% year-over-year from $1.356 billion in the same quarter last year.

Year-end backlog reached a record $7.8 billion, with $3.8 billion expected to be recognized over the next 12 months from remaining performance obligations. The company ended the quarter with approximately $3 billion in cash and cash equivalents.

“Strong customer demand for our expanding AI-driven product portfolio and the essential nature of Cadence’s engineering software position us well to capture the massive opportunities in the AI era,” said Anirudh Devgan, president and CEO.

Looking ahead, the company expects fiscal-year adjusted earnings per share from $8.05 to $8.15, versus the consensus estimate of $8.05. It anticipates revenue from $5.90 billion to $6.00 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $5.94 billion.

For the first-quarter, the company sees adjusted earnings per share from $1.89 to $1.95, versus the consensus estimate of $1.80.

Consensus Ratings And Analyst Action

The company has a consensus rating of Buy and a consensus price target of $366.50. Recent analyst action include:

Cadence Shares Climb Higher

CDNS Price Action: At the time of writing, Cadence stock is trading 5.43% higher at $298.83, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: EBay In Focus Ahead Of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY) is in the spotlight Wednesday ahead of the company’s fourth quarter earnings report after the market closes.

  • EBay stock is taking a breather. Where is EBAY stock headed?

Earnings Expectations, Recent Performance And What To Watch

Analysts estimate earnings per share of $1.35 and revenue of $2.88 billion. The company has beat estimates for earnings per share and revenue in four of the last four quarters. In its most recent quarter, reported on Oct. 29, the company posted earnings per share of $1.36, beating the consensus estimate of $1.33. In addition, it reported revenue of $2.82 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $2.73 billion.

Investors should keep an eye on EBay’s gross merchandise volume (GMV), which reflects overall sales activity on the platform, as it is a critical indicator of e-commerce health. Additionally, watch for trends in active buyer growth, which has been a focal point in EBay’s strategy to enhance user engagement and drive sales. Finally, monitor any shifts in average selling prices, which can impact revenue generation and margin stability.

Consensus Ratings And Analyst Action

EBay has a consensus rating of Neutral and a consensus price target of $92.93. Recent action include:

  • UBS analyst Stephen Ju maintained a Neutral rating on eBay and lowered the price target from $95 to $93.

Technical Indicators Reflect Bearish Pressure

Currently, EBay is trading 10% below its 20-day SMA and 8.6% below its 100-day SMA. Over the past 12 months, the stock has seen a growth of 14.56%, and it is now trading closer to its 52-week low of $58.71 than its high of $101.15. This positioning suggests a cautious market sentiment towards eBay in the longer term.

The RSI stands at 33.94, indicating a neutral market momentum, while the MACD shows a bearish trend with a reading of -2.2627 below the signal line at -0.8149. This suggests that despite the current premarket gains, there may be underlying bearish pressures affecting the stock.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for EBay, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 2.6) — The stock is trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality: Neutral (Score: 58/100) — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 25.81) — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: EBay’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a cautious outlook. While the Quality score indicates a stable financial base, the low Value and Momentum scores suggest that the stock may face challenges in outperforming the market in the near

EBay Shares Remain Flat

EBAY Price Action: At the time of writing, EBay shares are trading 0.36% higher at $80.32, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Tilray Lands Exclusive Carlsberg US Brewing Deal
data.description:
Tilray Brands, Inc. (NASDAQ:TLRY) shares are up during Wednesday’s premarket session following the announcement of an exclusive multi-year licensing agreement with the Carlsberg Group.

This partnership allows Tilray to produce, market, and distribute several Carlsberg-branded beers in the U.S., enhancing its position in the beverage market as it seeks to leverage its operational scale and expertise in the industry.

Under the terms of the agreement, Tilray has secured a multi-year license to produce and distribute Carlsberg, Carlsberg Elephant, 1664, and Kronenbourg 1664 Blanc beers across the United States starting January 1, 2027.

This collaboration is expected to bolster Carlsberg’s market share in the U.S., which is the second-largest beer market globally, and is seen as a strategic move to create long-term value for both companies.

Prinz Pinakatt, Chief Growth Officer of Tilray Beverages, stated, “Carlsberg’s portfolio aligns well with Tilray Beverages’ expanding platform. Our established capability to brew at scale, build brand equity, and commercialize effectively throughout the U.S. makes us an ideal partner to support Carlsberg’s growth objectives.”

Irwin D. Simon, Tilray’s CEO, highlighted that this collaboration aligns with their strategy of partnering with leading brands to maximize the value of their beverage operations.

Technical Analysis

Currently, the stock is trading 3.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 15.2% below its 50-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend in the short term. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 16.05%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing challenges in the market.

The RSI is at 42.07, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, the MACD is above its signal line, indicating a bullish signal that could suggest potential upward momentum if sustained.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while there is some upward pressure, the stock may require further strength to break through resistance levels.

  • Key Resistance: $8.50
  • Key Support: $7.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Tilray Brands is slated to provide its next financial update on April 7, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 1 cent (Down from $0.00)
  • Revenue Estimate: $203.24 million (Up from $185.78 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Roth Capital: Neutral (Lowers Target to $10.00) (January 20)
  • Jefferies: Buy (Raises Target to $200.00) (August 25, 2025)

Top ETF Exposure

  • Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (NYSE:MJ): 12.38% Weight

Significance: Because TLRY carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

TLRY Price Action: Tilray Brands shares were up 0.40% at $7.54 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo monticello via Shutterstock
